name: Brazil National Cybersecurity Policy (Decree No. 11,856/2023)
summary: Brazil's National Cybersecurity Policy establishes the strategic framework for protecting cyberspace and digital infrastructure across the country. It creates the National Cybersecurity Committee (CNCiber) to coordinate policy implementation, promote public-private collaboration, and develop cybersecurity capabilities for government and critical infrastructure sectors.
keyRequirements: National Cybersecurity Committee (CNCiber) established for policy coordination and oversight
Cybersecurity standards and guidelines for federal government information systems
Critical infrastructure protection framework across energy, telecommunications, and financial sectors
National cybersecurity incident response coordination through CERT.br and CTIR Gov
Promotion of cybersecurity education, workforce development, and research
International cooperation on cybersecurity threats and information sharing
penalties: The decree establishes policy framework rather than direct penalties. Enforcement is through sector-specific regulators. Federal agencies must comply with cybersecurity directives or face administrative accountability measures. Critical infrastructure non-compliance addressed through existing regulatory frameworks.
